title: Cloudflare Announces Third Quarter 2025 Financial Results
text: Third quarter 2025 revenue totaled $562.0 million, representing an increase of 31% year-over-year, with GAAP loss from operations of $37.5 million and non-GAAP income from operations of $85.9 million. Non-GAAP EPS was $0.27, which exceeded analyst estimates of $0.23, representing a 15.4% beat. Cloudflare delivered RPO year-over-year growth of 43% and Current RPO year-over-year growth of 30%, which substantially outpaced the 31% year-over-year recognized revenue growth and signals strong forward acceleration in new bookings driven by success in landing larger, long-term enterprise contracts. The company reported a 23% increase in large customers, with revenue contribution from these customers growing 42% year-over-year. Dollar-based net retention rate improved to 119%, up 5 percentage points quarter-over-quarter. Cash, cash equivalents, and available-for-sale securities were $4,043.5 million as of September 30, 2025. Cloudflare raised guidance for full-year 2025, now anticipating revenues between $2,142 million and $2,143 million, up from the previous guidance range of $2,113.5-$2,115.5 million. CJ Desai, who held a key executive role, is leaving to become CEO of another technology company.
